---
title: "Heart Disease"
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output:
  html_document:
    number_sections: true
    fig_caption: true
    toc: true
    fig_width: 7
    fig_height: 4.5
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: hide
---

![](https://www.coulditbehcm.com/assets/buildeasy/us-commercial/coulditbehcm/en/images/tabs/tabs-non-obstructive-hcm.gif)


# Introduction  {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

The goal of this competition is to predict heart disease.

My notebook serves as a demonstration of some of the possible techniques available to arrive at a solution.  I intend to add to this as I have time available. Your questions and comments are welcome.

Lets dive right in.

The Kaggle kernels have many of the common r packages built in.  

## Load libraries

```{r }
#| label: setup
#| warning: false
#| message: false

if (dir.exists("/kaggle")){
  path <- "/kaggle/input/playground-series-s6e2/"
    
# options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/2021-03-22"))

install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("bonsai")
pak::pak("ggplot2")
    
remotes::install_github("luisdva/hexsession", quiet = TRUE)    
remotes::install_url('https://github.com/catboost/catboost/releases/download/v1.2.8/catboost-R-linux-x86_64-1.2.8.tgz', 
                     INSTALL_opts = c("--no-multiarch", "--no-test-load"),
                    quiet = TRUE)  
    
cores <- future::availableCores()

} else {
  path <- stringr::str_c(here::here("data"),"/")
  orig_path <- stringr::str_c(here::here("data"),"/")

  cores <- future::availableCores(omit = 2)
  
}
 
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library(tidyverse, quietly = TRUE) # metapackage of all tidyverse packages
library(tidymodels, quietly = TRUE) # metapackage see https://www.tidymodels.org/

library(correlationfunnel)

library(bonsai)  # Tidymodels access to lightgbm
library(catboost) # Catboost
library(stacks) # ensembling    
library(DALEXtra) # Tidymodels Explainability         

# library(mirai)
# daemons(cores)
    
})

conflicted::conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflicted::conflict_prefer("where", "dplyr")


tidymodels_prefer()

options(tidymodels.dark = TRUE)

theme_set(theme_minimal())

hexsession::make_tile(packages = c("lightgbm","bonsai", "catboost", "DALEXtra", "DALEX",  "correlationfunnel","yardstick","workflows",
                                   "tune","rsample","recipes","parsnip","dials","broom","tidymodels","lubridate",
                                   "forcats","stringr","dplyr","purrr","readr","tidyr","tibble","ggplot2","tidyverse"),
                   #  highlight_mode = TRUE
                     )


```



## Load Data

```{r }
#| label: load data
#| warning: false
#| message: false
preprocessor <- function(dataframe) {

dataframe <- dataframe |>
    janitor::clean_names() |> 
    
    mutate(sex = factor(sex),
           chest_pain_type = factor(chest_pain_type),
           ekg_results = factor(ekg_results),
           exercise_angina = factor(exercise_angina),
           fbs_over_120 = factor(fbs_over_120),
           thallium = factor(thallium),
           st_depression = factor(st_depression)) |> 
  
    mutate(across(c(where(is.character)), \(x) as.factor(x)))  

return(dataframe)
}

raw_df <- read_csv(str_c(path, "train.csv"),
                   show_col_types = FALSE) 

tst_df <- read_csv(str_c(path, "test.csv"),
                   show_col_types = FALSE)  

all_df <-
    bind_rows(raw_df %>% mutate(source = "train"),
              tst_df %>% mutate(source = "test")) |>
          preprocessor() 

train_df <- all_df %>% 
  dplyr::filter(source == "train") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-source) 

competition_df <- all_df %>% 
  dplyr::filter(source == "test") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-source, -heart_disease)


```




## Features

We will de-duplicate the training set and use the first heart_disease data point.

```{r}
#| label: Features
#| warning: false
#| message: false



features <- train_df %>%
  dplyr::select(-id, -heart_disease) |> 
  names()

train_df <- train_df %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(pick({{features}})) |> 
  dplyr::summarise(id = min(id),
                   heart_disease = first(heart_disease),
                   .groups = "drop")

nom_features <- train_df |> 
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(features)) |> 
  dplyr::select(dplyr::where(is.factor)) |> 
  names() 

logical_features <- train_df |> 
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(features)) |> 
  dplyr::select(dplyr::where(is.logical)) |> 
  names() 

num_features <- train_df |> 
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(features)) |> 
  dplyr::select(dplyr::where(is.numeric)) |> 
  names()
```

Nominal features:

`r nom_features`

Numeric features: 

`r num_features`

Logical features: 

`r logical_features`


Size of the combined train and competition datasets:

`r nrow(all_df)`

Size of training set after de-duplication

`r nrow(train_df)`



# EDA {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

## Numeric features


```{r}
#| label: numeric
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig.height: 6

train_df %>% 
  dplyr::select(all_of(num_features), heart_disease ) %>% 
  pivot_longer(-heart_disease,
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(value, fill = heart_disease)) +
  geom_density(position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_brewer(type = "seq", palette = "YlOrRd") +
  facet_wrap(vars(metric), scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0, size = rel(0.5), margin = margin_part(b = 5, l = -10)),
    strip.clip = "off",
    panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),
    
        legend.position = "top") +
  labs(color = NULL, fill = "heart_disease",
       title = "Numeric Feature Univariate Distributions",
       caption = "Data: Kaggle | Visual: Jim Gruman")


```


## Nominal and Logical features

Explore the distribution of outcome class by factor level.


```{r}
#| label: nominal
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-height: 12

if(length(nom_features) >0){

train_df %>% 
  select(all_of(nom_features), all_of(logical_features), heart_disease) %>% 
  mutate(across(nom_features, fct_lump_n, n = 5, other_level = 'other', ties.method = "first"),
         across(logical_features, \(x) factor(x))) %>%
  pivot_longer(-heart_disease,
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
    
  filter(!is.na(heart_disease)) %>% 
    
  summarise(n = n(),
            .by = c(heart_disease, metric, value)) %>%
      
  mutate(value = tidytext::reorder_within(value, n, metric)) %>%
    
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = value, fill = heart_disease)) +
  geom_col() +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  scale_x_continuous(n.breaks = 3, guide = guide_axis(n.dodge = 2))  +
  scale_fill_brewer(type = "seq", palette = "YlOrRd") +
  facet_wrap(vars(metric), scales = "free", ncol = 2) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
  #  strip.text = element_text(margin = margin_part(b = -20, l = 8)),
  #  strip.clip = "off",
  #  panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),
       legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(title = "Nominal Feature Counts",
       fill = NULL, y = NULL,
       caption = "Data: Kaggle | Visual: Jim Gruman")

}

```

## Counts of Missingness

                  
```{r}
#| label: counts of missingness

train_df %>% 
  summarize(across(all_of(features), function(x) sum(is.na(x)))) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything(),
              names_to = "feature",
              values_to = "Count of Missing") %>% 
                   knitr::kable()

competition_df %>% 
  summarize(across(all_of(features), function(x) sum(is.na(x)))) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything(),
              names_to = "feature",
              values_to = "Count of Missing") %>% 
                   knitr::kable()

```

## Counts of Distinct
                   
               
```{r}
#| label: counts of distinct
               
train_df %>%
  summarize(across(all_of(features), n_distinct)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "feature", values_to = "Count of distinct train") |>
  left_join(
    competition_df %>%
      summarize(across(all_of(features), n_distinct)) %>%
      pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "feature", values_to = "Count of distinct test"),
    by = join_by(feature)
  ) %>% 
                   knitr::kable()
               
```

## Duplicated

Is this competition transaction already in the training data with a correct label?


```{r}
#| label: duplicates
#| warning: false
#| message: false

bind_rows(train_df %>% mutate(source = "train"),
              competition_df %>% mutate(source = "test")) |> 
    group_by_at(features) %>%
    mutate(num_dups = n(),
           dup_id = row_number()) %>% 
    ungroup() %>% 
    group_by(source) %>%
    mutate(is_duplicated = dup_id > 1) %>% 
    count(is_duplicated) %>% 
                   knitr::kable()



```


## Pairwise Correlations
                   
`ggcorrplot` provides a quick look at numeric features where the correlation may be significant. 

```{r}
#| label: pairwise correlations
#| fig-width: 9

trainp.mat <- train_df %>% 
  mutate(heart_disease = as.numeric(heart_disease)) |> 
  select(all_of(num_features), heart_disease) %>% 
  ggcorrplot::cor_pmat() 
                   
train_df %>% 
  mutate(heart_disease = as.numeric(heart_disease)) |> 
  select(all_of(num_features), heart_disease) %>%                    
  cor() %>% 
  ggcorrplot::ggcorrplot(hc.order = TRUE, 
                         lab = TRUE,
                         p.mat = trainp.mat,
    type = "lower", insig = "blank") +
  labs(title = "Pairwise Correlations Training Set")
                   
competitionp.mat <- competition_df %>% 
  select(all_of(num_features)) %>% 
  ggcorrplot::cor_pmat() 
                   
competition_df %>% 
  select(all_of(num_features)) %>%                   
  cor()   %>% 
  ggcorrplot::ggcorrplot(hc.order = TRUE, lab = TRUE,
                         p.mat = competitionp.mat,
    type = "lower", insig = "blank") +
   labs(title = "Pairwise Correlations Competition Set")

```                    

## Correlation Funnel


```{r}
#| label: correlation funnel 
#| warning: false
#| message: false


y_df <- train_df |>
  transmute(heart_disease = as.numeric(heart_disease)) 

train_df %>%
  select(all_of(num_features)) %>%
  correlationfunnel::binarize(one_hot = TRUE, n_bins = 3) %>%
  bind_cols(y_df) |>
  correlate(target = heart_disease) %>%
  plot_correlation_funnel()

``` 
                   
                   
## Target

Training scores range from 19.5 to 100, with many of them at either extreme.

```{r}
#| label: outcome 
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig.width: 6

train_df %>%
  summarize(outcome_sum = n(), .by = heart_disease) %>%
  arrange(desc(outcome_sum)) %>%
  mutate(prop = outcome_sum / nrow(train_df)) %>%
  mutate(ypos = cumsum(prop) - 0.5 * prop) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = prop, fill = heart_disease)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           width = 1,
           show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = ypos,
      label= paste0(heart_disease
                     , "\n", round(prop, 2) * 100, "%")
    ),
    color = "white",
    nudge_x = 0,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(type = "qual", palette = "Dark2") +                   
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank()  ) +
  labs(title = "Heart Disease", caption = "Data: Kaggle.com | Visual: Jim Gruman")

``` 
                
# Machine Learning {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}


## Recipe

```{r}
#| label: recipe
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig.width: 6

if (file.exists("/kaggle/working/classification_fit.rds")){             
train_df <- train_df |> predict(classification_fit, train_df) |>  
      transmute(past_run = .pred_Presence)  |> 
    bind_cols(train_df) 

rec <- recipe(formula(paste0("heart_disease ~ ", str_c(features, "past_run", collapse = " + "))), data = train_df)      
} else {
rec <- recipe(formula(paste0("heart_disease ~ ", str_c(features, collapse = " + "))), data = train_df)      
    
}
    
folds <- vfold_cv(train_df,
                  v = 3,
                  repeats = 1,
                  strata = heart_disease)
```


## Machine Learning 


```{r}
#| label: ml setup
#| warning: false
#| message: false


boost_tree_catboost_spec <- 
  boost_tree(
    trees = 100,
    tree_depth = tune(),
    learn_rate =  tune()
  ) %>% 
  set_engine(engine = "catboost" ,
         #     early_stopping_rounds = 350,
             od_type="Iter",
              use_best_model = TRUE,
             thread_count = cores) %>%  
  set_mode(mode = "classification")         
                   
dep_models <-
   workflow_set(
      preproc = list(base = rec),
      models = list(catboost = boost_tree_catboost_spec),
      cross = FALSE
   ) %>%
  option_add_parameters() |>
  option_add(
    control = finetune::control_sim_anneal( save_pred = TRUE, save_workflow = TRUE, verbose = TRUE, verbose_iter = TRUE),
    metrics = metric_set(roc_auc)
  )

catboost_params <- dep_models |>
 extract_workflow("base_catboost") |>
 extract_parameter_set_dials() |>
 update(
         tree_depth = tree_depth(range = c(6, 12)),
         learn_rate = learn_rate(range = c(-2.5,-1.0))
 ) 

catboost_params
                   
```

```{r}                   
#| label: ml workflow execution
                   
all_models <- dep_models |>
 option_add(
   param_info = catboost_params,
   id = "base_catboost"
 ) |>
  workflow_map("tune_sim_anneal", 
               resamples = folds, 
               iter = 5,
               metrics = metric_set(roc_auc), verbose = TRUE)     

                   
```                   

```{r}   
#| label: ml workflow results
                   
autoplot(all_models) +
  geom_text(aes(y = mean -0.002, label = wflow_id), angle = 90, hjust = 1)+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,NA), expand = c(0, 0)) |>
  theme(legend.position = "none")

rank_results(all_models, rank_metric = "roc_auc", select_best = TRUE) %>%
   select(rank, mean, model, wflow_id, .config) |> 
   arrange(desc(mean))
```
```{r}   
#| label: ml ensemble
                   
dep_stack <- stacks() %>%
  add_candidates(all_models) %>%
  blend_predictions(  metric =  metric_set(roc_auc),
      penalty = c(10^seq(-4.7, -3.3, 0.1)),
      non_negative = TRUE,
      control = tune::control_grid(allow_par = TRUE))

autoplot(dep_stack)

autoplot(dep_stack, type = "members")        
                   
autoplot(dep_stack, "weights")
                   
classification_fit <- dep_stack %>% 
     fit_members()    

```



# Performance {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r}
#| label: explainer
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig.height: 12
                   
                   
explainer <- 
  explain_tidymodels(
    classification_fit, 
    data = train_df %>% dplyr::select(all_of(features)), 
    y = as.numeric(train_df$heart_disease),
    label = "Ensemble",
    verbose = FALSE
  )  %>% 
  model_parts()

ggplot_imp <- function(...) {
  obj <- list(...)
  metric_name <- attr(obj[[1]], "loss_name")
  metric_lab <- paste(metric_name, 
                      "after permutations\n(higher indicates more important)")
  
  full_vip <- bind_rows(obj) %>%
    filter(variable != "_baseline_")
  
  perm_vals <- full_vip %>% 
    filter(variable == "_full_model_") %>% 
    group_by(label) %>% 
    summarise(dropout_loss = mean(dropout_loss))
  
  p <- full_vip %>%
    filter(variable != "_full_model_") %>% 
    mutate(variable = fct_reorder(variable, -dropout_loss)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(dropout_loss, variable)) 
  
  if(length(obj) > 1) {
    p <- p + 
      facet_wrap(vars(label)) +
      geom_vline(data = perm_vals, aes(xintercept = dropout_loss, color = label),
                 linewidth = 1.4, lty = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_boxplot(aes(color = label, fill = label), alpha = 0.2)
  } else {
    p <- p + 
      geom_vline(data = perm_vals, aes(xintercept = dropout_loss),
                 linewidth = 1.4, lty = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "#91CBD765", alpha = 0.4)
    
  }
  p +
    theme(legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = metric_lab, 
         y = NULL,  fill = NULL,  color = NULL)
}
                   
ggplot_imp(explainer)      
                       

```                      


                   
# Submission
                   

```{r}
#| label: submission
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig.height: 6
#| fig.width: 6   

write_rds(classification_fit, "/kaggle/working/classification_fit.rds")
                                   
predict(classification_fit, train_df, type = "prob") %>% 
  bind_cols(train_df) |> 
  mutate(
    .pred_class = factor(if_else(.pred_Absence < 0.5, "Absence","Presence"))
  ) |>                    
  conf_mat(heart_disease, .pred_class) %>%
  yardstick:::autoplot.conf_mat(type = "heatmap") +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdPu")

predict(classification_fit, train_df, type = "prob") %>% 
  bind_cols(train_df) |> 
  yardstick::roc_auc(heart_disease, .pred_Absence)
                   
submit_df <- predict(classification_fit, competition_df, type = "prob") |>
  bind_cols(competition_df) |>

  transmute(
   id,
  `Heart Disease` = .pred_Presence)

head(submit_df)  %>% 
     knitr::kable()      

submit_df  %>% 
  write_csv("submission.csv")
```
